message: fix: guard against jq failure, atomic write, anchor .git removal in lib.sh

files.patch:
@@ -33,7 +33,10 @@ write_state() {
   local session_id="$1"
   local content="$2"
   mkdir -p "$_STATE_DIR"
-  printf '%s\n' "$content" > "$(_state_file "$session_id")"
+  local target
+  target=$(_state_file "$session_id")
+  local tmp="${target}.tmp.$$"
+  printf '%s\n' "$content" > "$tmp" && mv "$tmp" "$target"
 }
 
 get_context_id() {
@@ -69,7 +72,8 @@ buffer_tool_call() {
   current=$(read_state "$session_id")
   [ -z "$current" ] && return 1
   local updated
-  updated=$(echo "$current" | jq --argjson tc "$tool_call_json" '.tool_calls += [$tc]')
+  updated=$(echo "$current" | jq --argjson tc "$tool_call_json" '.tool_calls += [$tc]') || return 1
+  [ -n "$updated" ] || return 1
   write_state "$session_id" "$updated"
 }
 
@@ -116,7 +120,7 @@ get_git_name() {
 get_project_name() {
   local cwd="$1"
   local remote
-  remote=$(git -C "$cwd" remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null | sed 's/.*\///' | sed 's/\.git//')
+  remote=$(git -C "$cwd" remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null | sed 's/.*\///' | sed 's/\.git$//')
   if [ -n "$remote" ]; then
     echo "$remote"
   else
